title: hieracium triangulare
intro: Hieracium triangulare, also known as Devil's-bit or Hieracium, is a perennial flowering plant native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa, characterized by its yellow flowers and distinctive triangular leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Hieracium triangulare include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal medicine, the plant can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and is often used to treat conditions such as eczema, acne, and skin infections.
